title: Ne Zha 2: A Record-Breaking Chinese Animated Film
text:
Ne Zha 2 is a 2025 Chinese animated fantasy adventure film directed by Jiaozi, serving as the direct sequel to the 2019 film Ne Zha. Based on the mythological character Nezha from Xu Zhonglin's 16th-century novel Investiture of the Gods, the film was released in China on January 29, 2025, coinciding with Chinese New Year.

Plot Summary: The story follows Ne Zha and Ao Bing after they are struck by divine lightning. Their bodies are destroyed, and Master Taiyi Zhenren uses the Seven-Colored Sacred Lotus to regenerate them. When Ao Guang (Ao Bing's father and Dragon King of the East Sea) mistakenly believes his son is dead, he orders an attack on Chentang Pass. The two protagonists must complete three trials to become immortals and earn a potion capable of restoring the Sacred Lotus and creating a permanent body for Ao Bing. The heavenly Chan sect, led by Master Wuliang, orchestrated the destruction of Chentang Pass and manipulated the Dragon Kings.

Box Office Performance: Global gross: $2.267 billion against an $80 million budget. Records broken: The film became the highest-grossing animated film ever, surpassing Inside Out 2. The film reached 1 billion yuan within three days of release in China and continued accumulating records throughout its theatrical run. First animated film to cross $2 billion; highest-grossing non-English language film; fifth-highest-grossing film of all time.

Critical Reception: Western critics were highly positive, with Rotten Tomatoes showing a 91% approval rating (80 critics). Chinese audiences responded enthusiastically, with Douban Movies scoring it 8.4/10, while averaging 9.7/10 on Maoyan and 9.8/10 on Taopiaopiao.

Production Details: Budget: 600 million yuan (most expensive Chinese animated film). Production time: Five years. Team size: Over 4,000 people across 138 animation companies. Visual elements: More than 2,400 shots with over 1,900 special effects shots. A direct sequel is currently in development.
